notes: LlamaIndex ReActAgent — Nemotron Super 120B (Nebius bf16). FunctionTool.from_defaults introspects type annotations automatically. Eval details: {'has_portfolio_value': True, 'has_table_content': True, 'has_account_info': True, 'has_holdings': True, 'has_markdown_structure': False, 'table_count_mentioned': True}
raw_output:
The document has been parsed successfully. The extracted text includes the full content of the Fidelity Investment Report for July 1–July 31, 2015. Several tables were identified in the output, including:

1. A table showing "Your Portfolio Value" with columns for "This Period" and "Year-to-Date", listing Beginning Portfolio Value, Additions, Subtractions, Transaction & Costs, Fees & Charges, Change in Investment Value, and Ending Portfolio Value.

2. A table under "Accounts Included in This Report" with columns for Page, Account Type/Name, Account Number, Beginning Value, and Ending Value, detailing GENERAL INVESTMENTS, PERSONAL RETIREMENT, and EDUCATION (529) ACCOUNTS.

3. An "Income Summary" table presented in HTML <table> format, with rows for Taxable (Dividends, Interest, Short-term Capital Gains, Long-term Capital Gains), Tax-exempt (Dividends, Interest), Tax-deferred, Return of Capital, Liquidations, and Total, followed by a "Top Holdings" subsection listing Johnson & Johnson, Apple Inc, NH Portfolio 2015 Delphi, Corp Jr Sb Nt Slm Corp, and Spi Lkd Nt (OSM) with their values and portfolio percentages.

4. An "Asset Allocation" table showing Domestic Stock (42%), Bonds (20%), Short-term (6%), Other (4%), and Foreign Stock (28%), with a breakdown by Asset Class.

All extracted text and tables are included in the parser's output above.
